# A practical guide to all of the files in a cuastom classic wordpress theme and how it works

# All the Files in a Custom Classic WordPress Theme – A Beginner’s Guide

Creating a custom theme is one of the most rewarding ways to learn how WordPress really works. It forces you to dig into PHP, HTML, CSS, and the WordPress template hierarchy, while giving you a finished product that you can tweak forever.  
This article walks through every file you’ll encounter in a “classic” (non‑block) WordPress theme, explains what each file does, shows how they work together, and highlights common pitfalls. By the end you’ll be ready to build your own theme from scratch or extend an existing one.

> **WordPress.org Docs** – The official reference for theme development: https://developer.wordpress.org/themes/

---

## 1. Folder Structure – What to Expect

A typical theme folder contains the following core files and directories:

```
mytheme/
├── style.css
├── index.php
├── functions.php
├── header.php
├── footer.php
├── sidebar.php
├── single.php
├── page.php
├── archive.php
├── search.php
├── 404.php
├── screenshot.png
└── assets/
    ├── css/
    ├── js/
    └── images/
```

> **Tip**: The `assets` folder is optional but keeps your code tidy.

Let’s explore each file in depth.

---

## 2. style.css – The Theme Header

### Purpose
- Contains the **theme header** (metadata) that WordPress reads to identify the theme.
- Holds your main stylesheet for styling the site.

### Header Format

```css
/*
Theme Name:   My Custom Theme
Theme URI:    https://example.com/my-custom-theme/
Author:       Jane Doe
Author URI:   https://example.com
Description:  A lightweight, responsive theme for beginners.
Version:      1.0.0
License:      GNU General Public License v2 or later
License URI:  https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-2.0.html
Text Domain:  mycustomtheme
Tags:         custom-background, custom-logo, featured-images
*/
```

- **Text Domain** is critical for internationalization (`__()` and `_e()` functions).
- The comment block must start with `/*` and end with `*/`; WordPress parses it line by line.

### CSS Basics

```css
/* Basic reset */
body, h1, h2, h3, p { margin:0; padding:0; }

/* Layout */
body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; line-height:1.6; }
header, footer { background:#333; color:#fff; padding:20px; }

/* Responsive container */
.container { width:90%; max-width:1200px; margin:auto; }
```

> **Practice**: Use `@media` queries to make the theme mobile‑friendly.

---

## 3. functions.php – Adding Theme Support

### Purpose
- Registers theme features (e.g., menus, widgets, post thumbnails).
- Enqueues scripts and styles.
- Hooks into WordPress actions/filters.

### Core Code

```php
<?php
// Exit if accessed directly
defined( 'ABSPATH' ) || exit;

// 1. Theme support
function mytheme_setup() {
    // Add support for post thumbnails
    add_theme_support( 'post-thumbnails' );

    // Register navigation menu
    register_nav_menus( [
        'primary' => __( 'Primary Menu', 'mycustomtheme' ),
    ] );

    // Add support for custom logo
    add_theme_support( 'custom-logo', [
        'height'      => 80,
        'width'       => 200,
        'flex-height' => true,
    ] );
}
add_action( 'after_setup_theme', 'mytheme_setup' );

// 2. Enqueue scripts and styles
function mytheme_assets() {
    wp_enqueue_style( 'mytheme-style', get_stylesheet_uri(), [], filemtime( get_template_directory() . '/style.css' ) );
    wp_enqueue_script( 'mytheme-navigation', get_template_directory_uri() . '/assets/js/navigation.js', [], '1.0', true );
}
add_action( 'wp_enqueue_scripts', 'mytheme_assets' );

// 3. Register widget areas
function mytheme_widgets_init() {
    register_sidebar( [
        'name'          => __( 'Sidebar', 'mycustomtheme' ),
        'id'            => 'sidebar-1',
        'description'   => __( 'Add widgets here.', 'mycustomtheme' ),
        'before_widget' => '<section id="%1$s" class="widget %2$s">',
        'after_widget'  => '</section>',
        'before_title'  => '<h2 class="widget-title">',
        'after_title'   => '</h2>',
    ] );
}
add_action( 'widgets_init', 'mytheme_widgets_init' );
```

### Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| Forgetting `wp_head()` in header or `wp_footer()` in footer | Add them; they’re required for plugins and core scripts. |
| Using `get_stylesheet_uri()` in child theme | Use `wp_enqueue_style( 'parent-style', get_template_directory_uri() . '/style.css' );` |
| Enqueuing scripts in the wrong hook | Use `wp_enqueue_scripts` for front‑end assets. |

---

## 4. header.php – The Site Header

### Purpose
- Contains the opening `<html>`, `<head>` tags, and the top navigation.

### Example

```php
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html <?php language_attributes(); ?>>
<head>
<meta charset="<?php bloginfo( 'charset' ); ?>">
<title><?php wp_title( '|', true, 'right' ); ?></title>
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<link rel="profile" href="http://gmpg.org/xfn/11">
<?php wp_head(); ?>
</head>
<body <?php body_class(); ?>>
<header class="site-header">
    <div class="container">
        <?php if ( has_custom_logo() ) : the_custom_logo(); endif; ?>
        <h1 class="site-title"><a href="<?php echo esc_url( home_url() ); ?>"><?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?></a></h1>
        <nav class="primary-nav">
            <?php
            wp_nav_menu( [
                'theme_location' => 'primary',
                'menu_id'        => 'primary-menu',
            ] );
            ?>
        </nav>
    </div>
</header>
```

### Practical Tips

- Use `body_class()` to get useful classes (e.g., `home`, `single-post`).
- Keep the header minimal; heavy scripts should be loaded in footer.

---

## 5. footer.php – The Site Footer

### Purpose
- Closes the main content area and adds a footer section.

```php
<footer class="site-footer">
    <div class="container">
        <p>&copy; <?php echo date( 'Y' ); ?> <?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?></p>
        <?php
        wp_nav_menu( [
            'theme_location' => 'footer',
            'menu_id'        => 'footer-menu',
            'container'      => false,
        ] );
        ?>
    </div>
</footer>
<?php wp_footer(); ?>
</body>
</html>
```

> **Common Mistake**: Forgetting `wp_footer()` can break plugin functionality.

---

## 6. index.php – The Fallback Template

### Purpose
- Acts as a catch‑all template if no more specific file exists.
- Usually contains the loop that displays posts.

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>

<div class="container">
    <?php if ( have_posts() ) : ?>
        <header class="page-header">
            <h1 class="page-title"><?php _e( 'Blog', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></h1>
        </header>

        <?php while ( have_posts() ) : the_post(); ?>
            <article id="post-<?php the_ID(); ?>" <?php post_class(); ?>>
                <h2 class="entry-title"><a href="<?php the_permalink(); ?>"><?php the_title(); ?></a></h2>
                <div class="entry-meta">
                    <?php echo get_the_date(); ?> | <?php the_author_posts_link(); ?>
                </div>
                <div class="entry-content">
                    <?php the_excerpt(); ?>
                </div>
            </article>
        <?php endwhile; ?>

        <?php the_posts_navigation(); ?>
    <?php else : ?>
        <p><?php _e( 'No posts found.', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></p>
    <?php endif; ?>
</div>

<?php get_sidebar(); ?>
<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### Key Points

- Always wrap the loop in `have_posts()`/`the_post()`.
- Use `post_class()` to get default WordPress classes (`post`, `category-news`).

---

## 7. page.php – Static Page Template

### Purpose
- Handles WordPress “Pages” (static content).

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>

<div class="container">
    <?php while ( have_posts() ) : the_post(); ?>
        <article id="post-<?php the_ID(); ?>" <?php post_class(); ?>>
            <h1 class="entry-title"><?php the_title(); ?></h1>
            <?php the_content(); ?>
        </article>
    <?php endwhile; ?>
</div>

<?php get_sidebar(); ?>
<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### Common Mistake

- Forgetting to call `the_content()` – the page content will never show.

---

## 8. single.php – Single Post Template

### Purpose
- Displays individual blog posts.

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>

<div class="container">
    <?php while ( have_posts() ) : the_post(); ?>
        <article id="post-<?php the_ID(); ?>" <?php post_class(); ?>>
            <h1 class="entry-title"><?php the_title(); ?></h1>
            <div class="entry-meta">
                <?php echo get_the_date(); ?> | <?php the_author_posts_link(); ?>
            </div>
            <div class="entry-content">
                <?php the_content(); ?>
            </div>
        </article>
    <?php endwhile; ?>

    <?php comments_template(); ?>
</div>

<?php get_sidebar(); ?>
<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### Practical Workflow

1. **Create a “post”** in the admin panel.
2. WordPress automatically loads `single.php`.
3. The loop outputs the post title, meta, and content.

---

## 9. archive.php – Archive Pages

### Purpose
- Handles category, tag, author, date archives.

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>

<div class="container">
    <?php if ( have_posts() ) : ?>
        <header class="archive-header">
            <h1 class="archive-title"><?php post_type_archive_title(); ?></h1>
        </header>

        <?php while ( have_posts() ) : the_post(); ?>
            <article id="post-<?php the_ID(); ?>" <?php post_class(); ?>>
                <h2 class="entry-title"><a href="<?php the_permalink(); ?>"><?php the_title(); ?></a></h2>
                <div class="entry-meta"><?php echo get_the_date(); ?></div>
                <?php the_excerpt(); ?>
            </article>
        <?php endwhile; ?>

        <?php the_posts_navigation(); ?>
    <?php else : ?>
        <p><?php _e( 'No posts found.', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></p>
    <?php endif; ?>
</div>

<?php get_sidebar(); ?>
<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### Decision Point

- **Use `archive.php`** if you want a single layout for all archives.
- Create specific files (`category.php`, `tag.php`) if you need different designs.

---

## 10. search.php – Search Results

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>

<div class="container">
    <header class="search-header">
        <h1><?php printf( __( 'Search Results for: %s', 'mycustomtheme' ), get_search_query() ); ?></h1>
    </header>

    <?php if ( have_posts() ) : ?>
        <?php while ( have_posts() ) : the_post(); ?>
            <article id="post-<?php the_ID(); ?>" <?php post_class(); ?>>
                <h2 class="entry-title"><a href="<?php the_permalink(); ?>"><?php the_title(); ?></a></h2>
                <?php the_excerpt(); ?>
            </article>
        <?php endwhile; ?>

        <?php the_posts_navigation(); ?>
    <?php else : ?>
        <p><?php _e( 'No results found.', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></p>
    <?php endif; ?>
</div>

<?php get_sidebar(); ?>
<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### Practical Tip

- Add a search form in the header or sidebar using `get_search_form()`.

---

## 11. 404.php – Page Not Found

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>

<div class="container">
    <h1><?php _e( 'Page Not Found', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></h1>
    <p><?php _e( 'Sorry, but the page you were looking for doesn’t exist.', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></p>
    <?php get_search_form(); ?>
</div>

<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### Common Mistake

- Forgetting to style the 404 page – it can look unprofessional.

---

## 12. sidebar.php – Widget Area

```php
<aside class="sidebar">
    <?php if ( is_active_sidebar( 'sidebar-1' ) ) : ?>
        <?php dynamic_sidebar( 'sidebar-1' ); ?>
    <?php else : ?>
        <p><?php _e( 'Add widgets to the sidebar.', 'mycustomtheme' ); ?></p>
    <?php endif; ?>
</aside>
```

### Workflow

1. Register sidebar in `functions.php`.
2. Drag widgets into “Appearance → Widgets”.
3. The sidebar displays automatically on pages that call `get_sidebar()`.

---

## 13. screenshot.png – Theme Preview

- WordPress uses this image in the admin theme selector.
- Keep it 880 × 660 pixels for best results.

---

## 14. assets/ – Organizing CSS, JS, Images

### Example Structure

```
assets/
├── css/
│   └── main.css
├── js/
│   ├── navigation.js
│   └── custom.js
└── images/
    └── logo.png
```

### Enqueuing Assets

```php
function mytheme_assets() {
    wp_enqueue_style( 'mytheme-main', get_template_directory_uri() . '/assets/css/main.css' );
    wp_enqueue_script( 'mytheme-navigation', get_template_directory_uri() . '/assets/js/navigation.js', [], null, true );
}
add_action( 'wp_enqueue_scripts', 'mytheme_assets' );
```

> **Tip**: Use `filemtime()` for cache busting during development.

---

## 15. Practical Workflow – Building a Theme from Scratch

1. **Set up the folder**: `wp-content/themes/mycustomtheme`.
2. **Create style.css** with header.
3. **Add functions.php** – register support, enqueue assets.
4. **Create header.php & footer.php** – include `wp_head()`/`wp_footer()`.
5. **Add index.php** – implement the loop.
6. **Create page.php, single.php, archive.php, search.php, 404.php** – copy index and adjust.
7. **Add sidebar.php** – register widget area.
8. **Upload assets** – CSS, JS, images.
9. **Activate theme** in the admin panel.
10. **Test**: Visit front‑end, check navigation, search, archives.

---

## 16. Key Decisions & Best Practices

| Decision | Why It Matters |
|----------|----------------|
| **Use `get_template_part()`** | Keeps templates DRY; e.g., create `_content.php` for post content. |
| **Use `wp_nav_menu()`** | Enables menu editing in the admin panel. |
| **Add `post-thumbnails` support** | Allows featured images; use `the_post_thumbnail()` in templates. |
| **Add custom logo support** | Gives site owners a brand‑friendly header. |
| **Use `wp_enqueue_scripts`** | Prevents conflicts and ensures proper script loading order. |
| **Add `body_class()`** | Provides CSS hooks for page types (home, single, archive). |
| **Internationalize strings** | Wrap all text in `__()` or `_e()`. |

---

## 17. Common Mistakes & How to Avoid Them

| Mistake | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| **Missing `wp_head()`** | Add it in header after meta tags. |
| **Not calling `get_footer()`** | Always include at the bottom of templates. |
| **Hard‑coding URLs** | Use WordPress functions (`home_url()`, `site_url()`). |
| **Using PHP short tags** | Avoid `<?` – always use full `<?php`. |
| **Enqueuing styles in the wrong hook** | Use `wp_enqueue_scripts` for front‑end assets. |
| **Failing to escape output** | Use `esc_html()`, `esc_url()` where needed. |

---

## 18. A Realistic Example – “Blogify” Theme

Let’s walk through a minimal theme called **Blogify** that demonstrates the concepts above.

### 18.1 Directory

```
blogify/
├── style.css
├── functions.php
├── header.php
├── footer.php
├── index.php
├── single.php
├── page.php
├── archive.php
├── search.php
├── 404.php
├── sidebar.php
└── assets/
    ├── css/
    │   └── main.css
    └── js/
        └── navigation.js
```

### 18.2 style.css

```css
/*
Theme Name:   Blogify
Author:       John Smith
Description:  A clean, responsive blog theme.
Version:      1.0
Text Domain:  blogify
*/
body { margin:0; font-family:"Segoe UI",Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif; line-height:1.6; }
header, footer { background:#222; color:#fff; padding:20px 0; text-align:center; }
.container { width:90%; max-width:960px; margin:auto; }
h1, h2, h3 { color:#333; }
a { color:#0066cc; text-decoration:none; }
a:hover { text-decoration:underline; }
```

### 18.3 functions.php

```php
<?php
add_action( 'after_setup_theme', function() {
    add_theme_support( 'post-thumbnails' );
    register_nav_menus( [ 'primary' => __( 'Primary Menu', 'blogify' ) ] );
} );

add_action( 'wp_enqueue_scripts', function() {
    wp_enqueue_style( 'blogify-style', get_stylesheet_uri(), [], filemtime( __DIR__ . '/style.css' ) );
    wp_enqueue_script( 'blogify-nav', get_template_directory_uri() . '/assets/js/navigation.js', [], null, true );
} );

add_action( 'widgets_init', function() {
    register_sidebar( [
        'name'          => __( 'Sidebar', 'blogify' ),
        'id'            => 'sidebar-1',
        'before_widget' => '<section id="%1$s" class="widget %2$s">',
        'after_widget'  => '</section>',
        'before_title'  => '<h2 class="widget-title">',
        'after_title'   => '</h2>',
    ] );
} );
```

### 18.4 header.php

```php
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html <?php language_attributes(); ?>>
<head>
<meta charset="<?php bloginfo( 'charset' ); ?>">
<title><?php wp_title( '|', true, 'right' ); ?></title>
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<?php wp_head(); ?>
</head>
<body <?php body_class(); ?>>
<header class="site-header">
    <div class="container">
        <h1><a href="<?php echo esc_url( home_url() ); ?>"><?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?></a></h1>
        <?php wp_nav_menu( [ 'theme_location' => 'primary', 'menu_id' => 'primary-menu' ] ); ?>
    </div>
</header>
```

### 18.5 footer.php

```php
<footer class="site-footer">
    <div class="container">
        &copy; <?php echo date( 'Y' ); ?> <?php bloginfo( 'name' ); ?>
    </div>
</footer>
<?php wp_footer(); ?>
</body>
</html>
```

### 18.6 index.php

```php
<?php get_header(); ?>
<div class="container">
    <?php if ( have_posts() ) : while ( have_posts() ) : the_post(); ?>
        <article id="post-<?php the_ID(); ?>" <?php post_class(); ?>>
            <h2 class="entry-title"><a href="<?php the_permalink(); ?>"><?php the_title(); ?></a></h2>
            <div class="entry-meta"><?php echo get_the_date(); ?> | <?php the_author_posts_link(); ?></div>
            <div class="entry-content"><?php the_excerpt(); ?></div>
        </article>
    <?php endwhile; else : ?>
        <p><?php _e( 'No posts found.', 'blogify' ); ?></p>
    <?php endif; ?>
</div>
<?php get_sidebar(); ?>
<?php get_footer(); ?>
```

### 18.7 single.php, page.php, archive.php, search.php, 404.php

- Copy `index.php` and modify the header titles or content structure as needed.
- For brevity, they’re omitted here but follow the same pattern.

### 18.8 sidebar.php

```php
<aside class="sidebar">
    <?php if ( is_active_sidebar( 'sidebar-1' ) ) : dynamic_sidebar( 'sidebar-1' ); endif; ?>
</aside>
```

### 18.9 assets/js/navigation.js

```js
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    const menu = document.getElementById('primary-menu');
    if (!menu) return;
    // Simple toggle for mobile
});
```

### 18.10 Testing

1. Activate **Blogify** in the admin panel.
2. Add a few posts, pages, and widgets.
3. Verify navigation, search, archives, and 404 page work.

---

## 19. Final Checklist Before Publishing

- [ ] All files have correct permissions (`644` for PHP, `755` for directories).
- [ ] `style.css` header is valid.
- [ ] `wp_head()` and `wp_footer()` are present.
- [ ] All strings are internationalized.
- [ ] Scripts and styles are enqueued with proper dependencies.
- [ ] The theme passes the **Theme Check** plugin (https://wordpress.org/plugins/theme-check/).
- [ ] No PHP errors or warnings appear on the front‑end.

---

## 20. Wrap‑Up

Creating a custom classic WordPress theme is a hands‑on way to understand the core of WordPress. By mastering the files listed above, you’ll be able to:

- Build a theme from scratch or extend an existing one.
- Add custom functionality via `functions.php`.
- Structure templates cleanly with the template hierarchy.
- Avoid common pitfalls that break themes.

Happy theming! 🎉
